name: Cefaclor
description: Semisynthetic, broad-spectrum antibiotic derivative of cephalexin.
indication: For the treatment of certain infections caused by bacteria such as pneumonia and ear, lung, skin, throat, and urinary tract infections.
toxicity: Symptoms of overdose include diarrhea, nausea, stomach upset, and vomiting.
mechanismOfAction: Cefaclor, like the penicillins, is a beta-lactam antibiotic. By binding to specific penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located inside the bacterial cell wall, it inhibits the third and last stage of bacterial cell wall synthesis. Cell lysis is then mediated by bacterial cell wall autolytic enzymes such as autolysins. It is possible that cefaclor interferes with an autolysin inhibitor.
pharmacodynamics: Cefaclor is a second generation cephalosporin antibiotic with a spectrum resembling first-generation cephalosporins. In vitro tests demonstrate that the bactericidal action of the cephalosporins results from inhibition of cell-wall synthesis. Cefaclor has been shown to be active against most strains of the following microorganisms, both in vitro and in clinical infections: Gram positive aerobes - Staphylococci (including coagulase-positive, coagulase-negative, and penicillinase-producing strains), Streptococcus pneumoniae, and Streptococcus pyogenes (group A ß-hemolytic streptococci). Gram-negative aerobes - Escherichia coli, Haemophilus influenzae (including ß-lactamase-producing ampicillin-resistant strains), Klebsiella sp, and Proteus mirabilis.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefaclor.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefaclor.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefaclor may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefaclor can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
